title: The Soul in the Face of Things — James Hillman and the Return of Anima Mundi
author: James Hillman

body.text: Hillman asks an unsettling question. What if the soul has never been confined inside us? What if psychic reality belongs to the world itself? Psychology became impoverished when it withdrew soul from things. The task is to recover the world'sown imagination — to perceive that every thing presents itself as an image with its own interiority.
Psychic reality is adimension of the world. The repression of the anima mundi is the central wound of modern consciousness.

Hillman'sreversal is profound — psychology usually begins with the individual psyche and projects outward. Hillman asks us to imagine the opposite. The world itself possesses psychic reality. Mountains, streets, buildings, weather, rivers, and abandoned objects cease to be neutral matter. They become presences that ask to be perceived, analysed.

body.caption: The soul of the world — landscape as psychological presence, not scenery.

body.text: In biblical psychology the heart is the organ that perceives the soul of the world.

Hillman links the heart to imagination in away that recalls Ficino and Corbin. The heart is acognitive organ — it perceives the world'sinterior. When the heart is open, things show their depth. Atree is abiomass and apresences. Astreet is asphalt and afate. The anima mundi returns through aesthetic perception: seeing the world as image, object.
The world is not only aphysical environment but also apsychological one. The soul of the world is the imaginative reality that things present when they are seen with the heart.

The imaginal becomes ecological. Every thing has aface — away it looks back at you. The practice is to stop treating the world as raw material and start attending to what it shows. The soul in the face of things asks to be recognised. Recognition is the first act of world-soul recovery.

body.caption: Every thing with a face — matter presenting its interior to the perceiving heart.

body.text: Hillman ends the exile of soul from world. Soul was never lost. It was misperceived. The world has been speaking all along in the language of image, presence, and face. The task is to stop analysing and start answering.
